title: NVIDIA/IsaacTeleop
abstract:
The unified framework for sim & real robot teleoperation

# Isaac Teleop **The unified framework for high-fidelity ego-centric and robotics data collection.** [ ](https://www.python.org/downloads/) [ ](https://isaac-sim.github.io/IsaacLab/develop) [ ](https://numpy.org/) [ ](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) --- ## Overview **Isaac Teleop**: The unified standard for high-fidelity egocentric and robot data collection. It is designed to address the data bottleneck in robot learning by streamlining device integration; standardizing high-fidelity human demo data collection; and foster device & data interoperability. ## Key Features - Unified stack for sim & real teleoperation - Standardized device interface - Flexible retargeting framework See the [Ecosystem](https://nvidia.github.io/IsaacTeleop/main/overview/ecosystem.html) page for supported robotics stacks, devices, and retargeting details. ### Teleoperation Use Cases - Currently supported use cases - Use XR headsets for gripper / tri-finger hand manipulation - Use XR headsets with gloves for dex-hand manipulation - Seated full body loco-manipulation (Homie) - Tracking based full body loco-manipulation (Sonic) - Egocentric data collection (aka “no-robot”) - Upcoming use cases - Teleoperate using only non-XR devices (e.g. gamepad, Gello, haply, etc.) - Teleoperate cloud based robotics simulations - Remote teleoperation with camera streaming to the desktop - Remote teleoperation with immersive camera streaming to XR headsets ## Quick Start ### Documentation Our [documentation page](https://nvidia.github.io/IsaacTeleop) provides everything you need to get started, including detailed tutorials and step-by-step guides. Follow these links to learn more: - [Architecture](https://nvidia.github.io/IsaacTeleop/main/overview/architecture.html) - [Quick installation steps](https://nvidia.github.io/IsaacTeleop/main/getting_started/quick_start.html) - [How to build from source](https://nvidia.github.io/IsaacTeleop/main/getting_started/build_from_source.html) ### Install & Run
abstract_zh: Isaac Teleop 是一个用于高保真自我中心视角和机器人数据采集的统一标准框架。它旨在通过简化设备集成、标准化高保真人类演示数据采集以及促进设备与数据互操作性，来解决机器人学习中的数据瓶颈问题。主要特性包括：仿真与真实遥操作的统一栈、标准化设备接口、灵活的重定向框架。当前支持的使用案例包括：使用XR头显进行夹爪/三指手操作、使用XR头显和手套进行灵巧手操作、坐姿全身移动操作（Homie）、基于追踪的全身移动操作（Sonic）以及自我中心数据采集（即“无机器人”）。即将支持非XR设备遥操作、云端机器人仿真遥操作、远程桌面遥操作以及远程沉浸式XR遥操作。
tldr_zh: 统一框架简化机器人遥操作与数据采集，解决数据瓶颈
cover_zh: 机器人数据采集太麻烦？这个统一框架让仿真与真实遥操作无缝衔接

judge.reason: NVIDIA官方遥操作框架，支持sim&real，集成多种设备与重定向，解决数据瓶颈。

>[!abstract] 一句话总结：构建统一框架，标准化设备接口并支持灵活的运动重定向，同时兼容模拟和真实环境下的遥操作。


# 一、动机

> 解决机器人学习中数据收集的瓶颈，通过统一的遥操作框架简化设备集成、标准化数据采集并提升互操作性。


# 二、方法

> 构建统一框架，标准化设备接口并支持灵活的运动重定向，同时兼容模拟和真实环境下的遥操作。


# 三、核心实验结果

> 支持XR头显、手套、全身追踪等多种设备，实现抓握、灵巧手操作、全身操作等用例，覆盖模拟和真实机器人数据采集。


# 四、对我的启发

> 与机器人具身智能直接相关，其架构思想可借鉴用于数据收集系统设计，但未提供具体性能指标。
